rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARILYN ANN MALLEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer To Daniel Mallen 1st Amended Verified Petition For: 1. Recovery Of Assets Of Decedent?S Estate Held By Defendants Or Any Of Them 2. Breach Of Fiduciary Duties As Agents Under Power Of Attorney 3. Accounting Of Transactions Taken Under Power Of Attorney 4. Financial Elder Abuse, And 5. Equitable Remedy Of Constructive Trust Filed By Kelli Mallen, Mary Mallen, William Mallen
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Before the Court is Respondents Kelli Mallen, Mary Mallen, and William Mallen's Demurrer to First Amended Verified Petition for (1) Recovery of Assets of Decedent's Estate Held by Defendants or Any of Them [Prob. Code sections 850, 856, 859]; (2) Breach of Fiduciary Duties as Agents Under Power of Attorney [Prob. Code section 4340 et seq.]; (3) Accounting of Transactions Taken by Agents Under Power of Attorney; (4) Financial Elder Abuse; and (5) Equitable Remedy of Constructive Trust ("First Amended Petition"), filed by Daniel Mallen on September 20, 2022, in both his individual capacity and as Administrator of the Estate of Marilyn Ann Mallen. The demurrer is brought against all causes of action for failure to state a cause of action, on the grounds that the claims are time-barred by either the three-year statute of limitations under Code of Civil Procedure section 338(d) for claims based on fraud or mistake, or by the four-year statute of limitations under Code of Civil Procedure section 343. At the November 14, 2022 hearing, the court granted Respondents' oral request to further extend the deadline to file a demurrer to the First Amended Petition, to December 12, 2022. On December 8, 2022, Respondent Kelli Mallen filed a declaration in support of automatic extension of the time to file demurrer. There was no proof of service filed with this declaration. The court did not respond to the request for extension made in the declaration. At the January 9, 2023 hearing, the court granted Respondents' oral request to further extend the deadline to file a demurrer to the First Amended Petition, to January 17, 2023. On January 19, 2023, Respondents filed a demurrer. The court acknowledges Petitioner's arguments made in the Opposition regarding the demurrer's procedural defects. The demurrer exceeds the statutory page limit (CRC 3.113(d)). It is untimely, even after the court granted a third filing deadline extension, to January 17, 2023. Despite these defects, the Court exercises its discretion to consider all of the papers filed in this demurrer proceeding, and enter a substantive ruling. Respondents are cautioned, however, procedural defects in filings may properly be the basis to deny a motion. Statutory procedural requirements must be followed by all parties to an action, as a function of due process and the efficient function of the court. The Court, on its own motion, takes Judicial Notice of the records of this court filed in Case Number PES-17-301322 and related Case Number PTR-18-302018. (Evid. C. section 452.) A demurrer is a pleading used to challenge the legal sufficiency of an opponent's pleading based on defects that appear either on the face of the pleading under attack or from matters outside the pleading that are judicially noticeable. (See Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal. 3d 311, 318; County of Fresno v. Shelton (1998) 66 Cal. App. 4th 996, 1008, 1009.) On August 21, 2022, Respondents' demurrer to the prior Petition filed by Daniel Mallen in both his individual capacity and as Administrator of the Estate of Marilyn Ann Mallen was sustained in part with leave to amend, on the basis that defects as to statutes of limitations appeared either on the face of the Petition, or from judicially noticeable matters. [Part 1 of 2]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARILYN ANN MALLEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Demurrer To Daniel Mallen 1st Amended Verified Petition For: 1. Recovery Of Assets Of Decedent?S Estate Held By Defendants Or Any Of Them 2. Breach Of Fiduciary Duties As Agents Under Power Of Attorney 3. Accounting Of Transactions Taken Under Power Of Attorney 4. Financial Elder Abuse, And 5. Equitable Remedy Of Constructive Trust Filed By Kelli Mallen, Mary Mallen, William Mallen (Added For The Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] While Respondents present argument in the moving papers and Reply to Opposition regarding the validity or accuracy of factual allegations in the First Amended Verified Petition, that is not the relevant analytical exercise on a demurrer. When considering a demurrer, the trial court must accept as true all material facts pleaded in the complaint and those arising by reasonable implication. (Moore v. Conliffe (1994) 7 Cal. 4th 634, 638; Montclair Parkowners Ass'n v. City of Montclair (1999) 76 Cal. App. 4th 784.) The First Amended Verified Petition survives the Demurrer, brought on the basis of the three-year statute of limitations under Code of Civil Procedure section 338(d), and the four-year statute of limitations under Code of Civil Procedure section 343, because it pleads specific material facts that, taken as true for the purpose of analysis on demurrer, support the First Amended Verified Petition's tolling and accrual claims regarding the applicable statutes of limitation. The issue of whether Petitioner will be able to support these claims with sufficient evidence, and whether Respondents will be able to rebut or defend against these claims with their own showing of sufficient evidence, is not before the court on a demurrer. Respondents Kelli Mallen, Mary Mallen, and William Mallen's Demurrer to First Amended Verified Petition for (1) Recovery of Assets of Decedent's Estate Held by Defendants or Any of Them [Prob. Code sections 850, 856, 859]; (2) Breach of Fiduciary Duties as Agents Under Power of Attorney [Prob. Code section 4340 et seq.]; (3) Accounting of Transactions Taken by Agents Under Power of Attorney; (4) Financial Elder Abuse; and (5) Equitable Remedy of Constructive Trust ("First Amended Petition") is OVERRULED. The Notice of Motion states Respondents "further move to strike on all Causes of Action, and the Prayer for; See Complaint pg. 29-30" (Notice at 1:27-28). At paragraph 7 of the Notice, Respondents state "Petitioner's demand for n accounting and alleged requests for accounting on either 9/11/2018 or 4/13/2021 should be dismissed because no such requests exist." (Notice at 2:26-28.) The Memorandum of Points and Authorities does not present argument in support of a motion to strike, and the motion is not captioned to indicate it is also a motion to strike. As far as this filing was intended to also be a motion to strike some or all of the First Amended Verified Petition, it is DENIED. Petitioner Daniel Mallen is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available consistent with San Francisco Superior Court Local Rules, rule 14.1(D). To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 2 of 2]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TRUST CREATED UNDER THE LAST WILL & TESTAMENT OF ETTA BACHARACH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of Account
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. A large unfiled document called "Accounting for the Time Period of February 7, 2019 Through February 6, 2021 and Petition for Settlement of the Same" as well as an unfiled pleading form of proof of service were received by this examiner. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 14.7 C.] A "courtesy copy" is an endorsed-filed, single-sided copy. [LRSF 14.7 B.1.] The Court's register of actions does not reflect this accounting as having been filed yet. As the accounting has not yet been filed, no hearing date has been set for it yet. The only correct form of notice in Probate proceedings of this sort is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. If a pleading form of proof of service is used, a copy of the Notice of Hearing that was served must be attached to it. Unlike Civil, all interested parties in Probate proceedings must receive direct notice. Trusts require 30 days' notice. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. PLEASE HAVE YOUR CASE NUMBER AND NAME READY FOR THE CLERK. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LEGACY ENHANCEMENT POOLED MINOR'S TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Directing Establishment Of Pooled Minor'S Trust; Authorization Of Trustee'S Fees; And Approving Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/21/2023, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues raised by the judicial officer after review and posted as the tentative ruling for the 12/06/2022 hearing: 1) Why is venue proper in San Francisco County, rather than Alameda County? The event that gave rise to the minor's claim occurred in Alameda County; the minor and Kaiser are both residents of Alameda County; the proposed trustee is a Texas corporation with its representative located in Pennsylvania; and there is no property of the proposed sub-account located in San Francisco County. If the Court is to approve this petition, the proposed sub-account with the pooled trust will be subject to continuing jurisdiction and supervision of the Court for many years until the minor attains the age of majority. 2) If the Court is to approve the proposed joinder and sub-account with the non-special needs pooled trust as prayed by this petition, what does the petitioner intend to do with Dept. 514's minor's compromise order directing distribution of partial settlement funds to a special needs pooled trust for the minor? 3) What is the initial amount of cash or other assets funding the proposed sub-account? 4) Why should the proposed joinder agreement and master pooled trust be governed by Texas law, rather than California law, even though the situs of the trust will be California, as so stated in the first amendment to the master pooled trust? An appearance may be required on 03/21/2023 on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar if no satisfactory progress is shown.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/24/23. (No earlier available date.) The wrong form of Notice of Hearing was served: DE-115 is required for a petition to determine claim to property. rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TRAYLOR TRUST, DATED MAY 3, 1996, AS PURPORTEDLY AMENDED AND RESTATED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Orders: 1. Invalidating The Purported Trust Based On Undue Influence And Lack Of Capacity; 2. Financial Elder Abuse; 3. Cancellation Of
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/12/2023. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 3) Direct notice must be given to Tamarror Traylor. CRC 7.51(a)(2). 4) Please state the petitioner's address. 5) Please state the address of Tamarror Taylor's residence of place or business. 6) Please provide a copy of a trustee notification served under Probate Code section 16061.7. If no notification has been served, please provide specific examples of the day-to-day activity of the trust carried on by Tamarror Traylor as trustee. 7) Are the petitioner and Tamarror Traylor at least 35 years old? 8) There is no conservatorship proceeding to confer concurrent jurisdiction on the probate court over the financial elder abuse claim under the Welfare and Institutions Code. See section 15657.3. 9) The petition was not filed under Probate Code section 850 for the probate court to adjudicate some causes of action normally raised in a civil action. See section 855. 10) Please provide a courtesy copy of Tamarror Traylor's objection filed on 02/24/2023 and proposed order. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
